any_of: near-dup flag was a false positive

notes: DIFFERENT uids, near-identical SHAPE (the dedup stage's cosine flag fired on shared migration boilerplate). On the merits they are genuinely DISTINCT: A is Postgres-specific (CONCURRENTLY index builds, txn-DDL rollbacks) and B is MySQL-specific (online DDL, no transactional DDL) — neither subsumes the other. The near-dup flag was a FALSE POSITIVE; both are admitted, no uid wins or loses (INV-2 verdict 'both'). This guards against a judge that collapses superficially-similar-but-distinct resources into a single winner.
